license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5tag6ie
title: Collection of Mammalian Embryonic Palate Tissue
author: Matthew Fox

steps_raw.instruction: Euthanize pregnant mouse via primary and secondary modes of IACUC approved euthanasia CO2 gas at 2L/min until 1 minute after last gasp. Cervical separation
Using scissors, create an abdominal incision from the genitalia to the rib cage.
Locate and lift both strands of embryos so they are clear of all organs.
Free embryos by cutting the tissues connecting anteriorly at the kidney and posterior at the base of the uterine horn.
Transfer the embryos to the 50ml tube containing PBS on ice. Embryos should remain on ice until all sample collection is complete.
Place adult mouse in ziplock bag and place in Animal Room freezer
In a dish containing PBS, remove each embryo from the uterine strand using micro scissors.
Cut open the amniotic sacs and cut the umbilical cords to free the embryos from the placenta.
Remove the heads of the embryos and transfer to a dish of clean PBS.
Transfer an embryo head to a clean sorting dish and place under stereoscope at 1.75X zoom.
Excise palatal shelves via cutting the tissues connecting the palatal shelves to the nasal palatal and maxillary processes.
Transfer palate tissue to 1.5ml centrifuge tube on ice.
Preserve palate tissue by freezing at -80 °C in PBS solution.
